phase_1.audio: Acoustic analysis reveals baseline of 55.4 dBA with periodic spikes up to 71.4 dBA throughout the 298s recording. Moderate noise levels present; some distraction possible but manageable.
phase_1.vitals: Vitals monitoring recorded 89 bpm as session average. This deviates from ideal focus-state parameters.
phase_1.gps_motion: GPS signal strength inconsistent at start (2.8m error margin), achieving 1.9m lock after stabilization. Kinetic data shows user establishing stable position within first seconds; minimal body movement thereafter.

phase_2.correlations: Positional settling (GPS convergence) corresponds to kinetic patterns. Acoustic consistency observed.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: Analysis indicates excellent concentration potential: quiet audio environment (61.4 dB), settled posture, and favorable physiology.

phase_3.recommendations: Current 298s sessions reasonable; consider testing longer 30-min blocks.

reason: Score determined by comprehensive assessment: ambient noise management (61.4dB), user-state stability (settled), physiological data (available), and sustainable duration (298s).
